title: HR for cardiovascular death with mineralocorticoid receptor antagonist in heart failure
primary_estimand: hazard ratio for cardiovascular death with MRAs

studies.name: Treatment of Preserved Cardiac Function Heart Failure With an Aldosterone Antagonist (TOPCAT)
The Effect Of Eplerenone Versus Placebo On Cardiovascular Mortality And Heart Failure Hospitalization In Japanese Subjects With Chronic Heart Failure

e156_body: In heart failure, does mineralocorticoid receptor antagonist compared with placebo change cardiovascular death? This synthesis aggregates 2 randomized trials of mineralocorticoid receptor antagonist drawn from the ClinicalTrials.gov AACT snapshot dated 2026-04-12. Trial-reported effect estimates were pooled on the log scale using a Paule-Mandel random-effects model with a prediction interval based on the t-distribution. The pooled HR for cardiovascular death was 1.30 (95% CI 0.51 to 3.34). Between-trial heterogeneity was I-squared 75 percent and the 95% prediction interval spanned 0.00 to 24873.64. These registry-derived data are consistent with a modest association, though the certainty remains uncertain and warrants cautious reading. Interpretation is limited by reliance on a single registry snapshot and by the small number of contributing trials, and does not generalise beyond the included trials.
